---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

1. One-sample t-test: two-sided, left tail, right tail

```{r}
cat("1)  One-sample t-test")
```

```{r}
set.seed(42)
x <- rnorm (25, mean = 5.2, sd =1.4)
mu0 <- 5.0
```

# single sample, two-sided, t-test

```{r}
# x = 25
b1 <- t.test(x, mu = mu0, alternative = "two.sided")
b1

#custom t-test
c1 <- one_sample_t_custom(x, mu = mu0, alternative = "two-sided")
```

```{r}
# custom function
one_sample_t_custom <- function (x, mu = 0,
                                 alternative = c("two.sided", "greater", "less")
```

```{r}
#left-tailed test: H1 < H0

mu0<5
b2 <- t.test(x, mu = mu0 , alternative = "less")
c2 
```

```{r}
#right-tailed distribution
```

```{r}
# Two sample t-test (unpaired)

xA <- rnorm(20, mean = 10, sd = 2)
yA <- rnorm(22, mean = 12, sd = 2.1)

#Welch (default)
bA_welch <- t.test(xA, yA, alternative = "two.sided", var.equal = FALSE)
cA_welch <- two_sample_t_custom(xA, yA, alternative = "two.sided", var.equal)
compare_t_results(bA_welch, cA_welch, "Two-sample unpaired, Welch (var.equal)")
```
